questions.question: In 1954, a small, lightweight Porsche racer called the 550 Spyder dominated a brutal open-road race through Mexico. The race was so infamous that its name stuck to Porsche's most famous performance sub-brand — still stamped on the back of 911s today. What is that nameplate?
questions.answerKey: Carrera — from the Carrera Panamericana, a 2,000-mile open-road race across Mexico (1950–1954). Porsche's 550 Spyder won its class in 1953 and 1954. Used on 911s ever since (911 Carrera, Carrera S, Carrera GT).
